changes_made.description: Added strong directive to write Node.js scripts instead of computing mentally. Added complete code templates with var declarations, parseCSV helper, and step-by-step workflow. Removed Python examples.
Shortened skill significantly to reduce token usage causing timeouts. Kept core code templates but removed verbose instructions.
Further reduced skill length. Simplified code templates. Used var declarations and field-by-field object construction to avoid qwen3-30b syntax errors with object literals.

Reverted to a style closer to the original skill (TypeScript code examples) but kept the critical directive to always write and execute a Node.js script. Removed CSV parsing helper that caused consistent {; syntax errors. Removed distance matrix and nearest point code templates to keep skill concise.

key_findings: qwen3-30b consistently generates 'var row = {;' instead of 'var row = {};' when copying parseCSV patterns from the skill
Longer skills cause timeouts because of increased token processing time for this model
The model can compute city-distance mentally but fails plate-boundary when attempting mental computation
Adding 'always write a script' directive fixed plate-boundary from 0 to 100
Balancing skill length vs. guidance detail is critical for smaller models
